title: Research Projects & Publications
text:
Full Publication List



2026 · First author


J-PAS: Semi-Supervised Sim-to-Obs Transfer for Robust Star–Galaxy–Quasar Classification
Daniel López-Cano, L. Raul Abramo, L. Nakazono, I. Pérez-Ràfols, et al.
arXiv preprint, arXiv:2602.13902, doi:10.48550/arXiv.2602.13902
Semi-supervised domain adaptation for robust star–galaxy–quasar classification, designed to transfer effectively from simulated to observational data in the J-PAS setting.


arXiv GitHub repository Google Slides Slides PDF







2026 · Collaboration


OJALÁ: Optimizing J-PAS Astronomy for Large-scale Analysis. A foundation model for the SED of galaxies, QSOs and stars
G. Martínez-Solaeche, R. M. González Delgado, R. García-Benito, …, D. López-Cano, et al.
arXiv preprint, arXiv:2604.00661, doi:10.48550/arXiv.2604.00661
A collaboration paper developing a foundation-model approach for spectral energy distributions of galaxies, QSOs, and stars in the J-PAS context.


arXiv







2024 · First author


Characterizing structure formation through instance segmentation
Daniel López-Cano, Jens Stüker, Marcos Pellejero Ibáñez, Raúl E. Angulo, Daniel Franco-Barranco
Astronomy & Astrophysics, 685, A37
This work explores structure formation in cosmological simulations through instance segmentation, connecting modern computer-vision techniques with questions in numerical cosmology.


Journal GitHub repository Google Slides Slides PDF







2024 · Co-author


An improved halo occupation distribution prescription from UNITsim Hα emission-line galaxies: conformity and modified radial profile
Guillermo Reyes-Peraza, Santiago Avila, Violeta Gonzalez-Perez, Daniel López-Cano, Alexander Knebe, Sujatha Ramakrishnan, Gustavo Yepes
Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 529, 3877–3893
A study of improved halo-occupation prescriptions for Hα emission-line galaxies, with attention to conformity and modified radial profiles.


Journal







2022 · First author


The cosmology dependence of the concentration–mass–redshift relation
Daniel López-Cano, Raúl E. Angulo, Aaron D. Ludlow, M. Zennaro, S. Contreras, Jonás Chaves-Montero, G. Aricò
Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 517, 2000–2011
A study of how halo concentrations vary across cosmologies, masses, and redshifts, contributing to the theoretical characterization of halo structure.


Journal Google Slides Slides PDF

UNITSIM-Galaxies: data release and clustering of emission-line galaxies
Alexander Knebe, Daniel López-Cano, Santiago Avila, Ginevra Favole, Adam R. H. Stevens, Violeta Gonzalez-Perez, Guillermo Reyes-Peraza, Gustavo Yepes, Chia-Hsun Chuang, Francisco-Shu Kitaura
Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 510, 5392–5407
A data-release and clustering analysis paper for emission-line galaxies in the UNITsim-Galaxies framework.


Journal

title: CV
text: I am a postdoctoral researcher in computational cosmology at the Institute of Physics of the University of São Paulo (IFUSP). My research combines cosmological simulations, machine learning for astrophysics, survey-oriented modelling, and scientific software development. I am also interested in the creation of pedagogical resources that leverage computational tools to produce interactive materials and exercises, with the aim of facilitating the learning of complex topics in theoretical physics and astrophysics.

title: Daniel López Cano
text:
Daniel López Cano


Postdoctoral Researcher in Computational Cosmology


Institute of Physics, University of São Paulo (IFUSP) · São Paulo, Brazil
dlopez@usp.br · daniellopezcano13@gmail.com

title: Daniel López Cano
text:
Research interests

Computational cosmology · machine learning for astrophysics · cosmological simulations · Ly\(\alpha\) forest and absorption processes · galaxy–halo connection · simulation-to-observation transfer· pedagogical resources for theoretical physics and astrophysics

title: Daniel López Cano
text:
About
Hello! I am a postdoctoral researcher in computational cosmology at the Institute of Physics of the University of São Paulo (IFUSP). My work brings together cosmological simulations, machine learning for astrophysics, survey-oriented modelling, and scientific software development. More recently, I have also been working on Ly\(\alpha\) absorption processes, fast skewer generation, and pedagogical resources that use computational tools to support the learning of theoretical physics and astrophysics.
I am currently a FAPESP Postdoctoral Fellow at IFUSP, and in 2026 I am also undertaking a FAPESP-BEPE visiting research stay at the Institut de Física d’Altes Energies (IFAE) in Barcelona. Before moving to São Paulo, I completed my PhD in Astrophysics at the Donostia International Physics Center (DIPC), after previous training in physics and theoretical physics at the Autonomous University of Madrid (UAM). Across these stages, I developed a broad interest in combining theoretical ideas, numerical methods, and data-driven techniques to study complex problems in cosmology and astrophysics.

title: Daniel López Cano
text:
Trajectory



FAPESP Postdoctoral Research Fellow
Institute of Physics, University of São Paulo (IFUSP) · São Paulo, Brazil
11/2024 – Present
Supervisor: Prof. Luis Raul Weber Abramo. Topics include cosmological simulations, semi-supervised machine learning techniques, Ly\(\alpha\) processes, and differential geometry.

IFUSP profile JPAS Domain Adaptation






FAPESP-BEPE Visiting Postdoctoral Research Fellow
Institut de Física d’Altes Energies (IFAE) · Barcelona area, Spain
02/2026 – 06/2026
Host supervisor: Prof. Andreu Font-Ribera. Co-mentor: Dr. Jonás Chaves-Montero. Project: SkewerJAX: fast Ly\(\alpha\) skewers and a survey-ready metal-contamination model.

FAPESP researcher page SkewerJAX






PhD in Astrophysics
Donostia International Physics Center (DIPC) / Universidad Autónoma de Madrid (UAM) · Donostia-San Sebastián / Madrid, Spain
09/2020 – 09/2024
Supervisor: Prof. Raúl E. Angulo. Thesis: Improving cosmological simulations: semi-analytic models, the internal structure of haloes, and machine learning techniques.

Thesis page Instance segmentation work SBI / BACCO workflows BACCO






MSc in Theoretical Physics
Institute of Theoretical Physics IFT UAM-CSIC · Madrid, Spain
09/2019 – 07/2020
Specialisation in Astrophysics and Physics of the Cosmos. Supervisor: Prof. Alexander Knebe.





BSc in Physics
Universidad Autónoma de Madrid · Madrid, Spain
09/2015 – 07/2019
Training in theoretical and experimental physics, including thesis work on galaxy statistics in cosmological simulations and tight-binding / evolutionary methods.
